notes: Prototype overlay only. Mission Control graph files were read, not modified.
Mission Control and Graphify are different ontologies today: document graph vs code/rationale graph.
Direct node merges are intentionally conservative; most nodes remain source-specific in this prototype.
Any `both` provenance in edges comes from transparent heuristic bridge edges, not hard proof of identity.
Graphify inferred edge direction appears reversed in raw `source`/`target` for some links; this prototype prefers `_src`/`_tgt` when present.
Merge quality is strongest for provenance preservation and weakest for semantic cross-source identity resolution.

Dream cycle brief — April 10, 2026
Dream cycle brief — April 11, 2026
Dream cycle brief — April 12, 2026. Kairos setup moved from hardware-waiting to SSH-proof waiting, Bianca's wedding shoe direction got clearer, and the local proxy/disks are still a mess.

Dream cycle brief — April 13, 2026. Apr 12 was mostly automation, but OFM gained a compliance gate, Kairos gained a weekly truth ledger, and Lagoon's pricing funnel got merged into one ship-ready page asset.

Dream cycle brief — April 14, 2026. Apr 13 was light on new conversation, but it added a sharper Alex Finn reliability pattern: heavy builder + light watchdog, which fits Kairos better than solo-agent heroics.
Dream cycle brief — April 15, 2026. The main pattern tonight was execution glue: several launch/sales/build threads gained the missing handoff layer that turns good strategy into something runnable.

Dream cycle brief — April 16, 2026. Apr 15 activity was real but split across sparse session visibility, so disk transcripts were used to recover the important threads: cadence recovery turned live, Astrid/Lagoon execution assets were already captured, and a few personal chat threads were filtered down to what actually matters.

Dream cycle brief — April 18, 2026. Apr 17 activity was very real despite sparse session API visibility: OFM gained a true Astrid launch gate, Kairos/cadence work hardened into a concrete drift punch list plus a real weekly status ledger, and workspace hygiene improved materially.

Dream cycle brief — April 19, 2026. Midnight activity was mostly systems work, but it materially advanced cadence recovery, refreshed Mission Control mirrors, and exposed a real session-store hygiene issue behind sparse session visibility.

Dream cycle brief — April 20, 2026. The day mixed real ops failures, a fresh OpenClaw hardware product idea, and more evidence that cron/session status must be verified against underlying reality rather than wrapper state.

Dream cycle brief — April 21, 2026. The day hardened the backup chain, verified cadence recovery against runtime, and extended weekly truth into the live week while session recovery still depended on disk transcripts.
